purpose: Pre-register the v3 E1 anchor design: fixed canonical 10 seeds × 4 models × 5 walk-forward folds × 2 paired universes (B 10-dim hc, C 51-dim Plan AAA top-15 members) at 21d horizon, with Hansen SPA + DM/HLN + transaction cost ladder + block bootstrap for multi-comparison and cherry-pick defense.

seeds.rationale: Same 10 seeds for ALL 4 models (paired comparison for DM/HLN tests; no per-model extension); canonical list per project rules; eliminates cherry-pick concern by reporting full distribution

models.drop_rationale_LSTM: LSTM sequence input cannot align cleanly with Universe B/C rolling-stats features. Feeding 21 days of pre-aggregated stats to LSTM produces highly autocorrelated time steps (only 1 day shift between consecutive steps), leaving LSTM almost no incremental temporal signal beyond MLP. Documented as paper §Limitations per plan §1.1 'LSTM limitation note'.
models.mamba_sage_status: OPTIONAL E5 only (plan §1.5); not part of E1 anchor; separate prereg if E5 runs

B.name: Universe B (10-dim hc)
B.source: run_step3_plan_z_part_a.py:101-117 (Plan Z++ universe; literature alphas + Phase 5 baselines, semantically de-duplicated 13→10)

C.name: Universe C (51-dim Plan AAA top-15 members)
C.feature_list_source: artifacts/plan_aaa/ranking.csv top 15 rows; union of all members across these 15 groups
C.slicing_method: by feature_name; column indices resolved at run-time from data/reference/sp500_5y_alpha158_features_meta.json feature_order

universes.paired_design_rationale: Test decisions.md:19/20 'feature richness threshold' hypothesis: at low-dim (B), MLP ≈ SAGE (decisions.md:19 p=0.679); at high-dim (D=168, decisions.md:20 p=0.005 SAGE > MLP). Universe C (51-dim) is intermediate and tests where the threshold lies on US S&P 500 at 21d horizon.

fold_dates.desc: Q2-2025 (known regime outlier — LOFO sensitivity required)

design.horizon_lock: locked per CLAUDE.md Rule 8 invariants; next-day close-to-close 21d forward return
design.label_construction: z_score = cross-sectional z(excess_return_21d), market-demeaned, day-wise std normalized; reused from run_step3_plan_z_part_a.py:122-128 / run_horizon_ablation.py:181-194

purge_embargo.between_fold_embargo_clarification: EXPANDING-WINDOW DESIGN — no between-fold gap required. Fold N+1's training set intentionally INCLUDES fold N's test period; this is by design, not a leak. The v2-era between-fold check `(fold_{N+1}.train_end - 42d) > fold_N.test_end` was conceptually backwards (always false because train_end shifts forward by quarters but test_end of prior fold is later) per Codex C-02; removed per Codex Round D D-02.
purge_embargo.explicit_contract_per_D-02: Within-fold purge is the PRIMARY leak prevention. For each fold N, drop the last 21 trading days from train AND val splits before extracting features. This guarantees: (a) max(train_label_end_date) < min(val_feature_date); (b) max(val_label_end_date) < min(test_feature_date). Assertion code documented in plan §1.8 — uses trading_dates array, computes last_train_label_end_date = trading_dates[last_train_feat_idx + HORIZON], compares to first_val_feat_date with strict `<`.
purge_embargo.sanity_check_must_be_executable: run_storya_e1_anchor.py start-of-run block runs the §1.8 plan assertion on actual trading_dates array; with the purge in place the assertion passes with a 1-trading-day gap; verification: removing the purge MUST cause the assertion to FIRE.

winsorization.method: Per-fold p1/p99 train-only winsor on features (NOT labels)
winsorization.applied_to: features only, both Universe B and C

scaling.method: Per-fold StandardScaler fit on train, applied to val + test

model_hparams.source_file: experiments/storya_e1_anchor/hp_grid.json (must be written alongside this prereg)
model_hparams.source_of_NN_defaults: archived/scripts/run_horizon_ablation.py PARAMS dict lines 54-64 (production defaults; no novel HP search)
model_hparams.source_of_LightGBM_defaults: Microsoft Qlib LGBModel defaults (preserves comparability with experiments/wf5_results.csv baseline)
model_hparams.rationale: Per Codex A-07: any HP variation inflates multi-testing ledger; locking to production defaults eliminates this risk

design.cell_id_formula: cell_id = universe_idx*200 + model_idx*50 + fold_idx*10 + seed_idx (range 0..399, globally unique)

graph.type: single-relation correlation graph (E1 anchor; sector/news edges in E3 and E4-α)
graph.construction: rolling 126d window of stock close-to-close returns, Spearman |ρ| > 0.6, monthly snapshot step=21d
graph.last_snapshot_constraint: ≤ end_of_day(t-1) for prediction at day t (PIT compliance)

post_processing.framework: Hansen SPA + DM/HLN + block bootstrap + transaction cost ladder (plan §1.4 v3)

hansen_spa.loss: -IC per candidate per day t, where candidates are seed-averaged per (model, universe)
hansen_spa.benchmark: LightGBM_price seed-averaged loss per day per universe
hansen_spa.M_count_semantics_per_D-04: M counts NON-baseline candidates only. Per universe: {GAT, SAGE-Mean, MLP} = 3 candidates vs LightGBM benchmark. Joint: 3 candidates × 2 universes = 6 candidates vs pooled LightGBM benchmark. Seeds are AVERAGED per (model, universe, date, fold) BEFORE entering the SPA loss matrix — consistent with DM_HLN seed_aggregation block. Prior v3 draft used M=40/80 (treating each (model, seed) as a candidate) which contradicted seed aggregation; corrected per Codex Round D D-04.

seed_aggregation.LOCKED_per_Codex_C-04: Average IC across 10 seeds per (model, date, fold) BEFORE forming DM series. Seeds are NOT pooled as independent observations.
construction: Step 1: per (model, seed, fold, date), compute IC_m_s_f_t
Step 2: per (model, fold, date), seed-average: IC_seed_avg_m_f_t = mean_s(IC_m_s_f_t)
Step 3: per (model, date), concatenate folds: IC_pooled_m_t = concat_f(IC_seed_avg_m_f_t)
Step 5: paired difference: d(t) = L_A(t) - L_B(t)
seed_aggregation.T_pooled_warning: If seeds were treated as independent observations (T=3130), HLN small-sample correction would silently assume a longer effective test series. This pathology is eliminated by seed-aggregation BEFORE DM.

DM_HLN.DM_formula: mean(d_t) / sqrt(NW_HAC_variance(d_t) / T) where T = 313 seed-aggregated days
DM_HLN.HLN_correction: DM × sqrt((T + 1 - 2h + h(h-1)/T) / T) with h=21, T=313, factor≈0.935
DM_HLN.multiple_testing: BH-FDR at q=0.05 over combined 5-test family (3 vs LGB + 2 vs MLP); 7 if E5 OPTIONAL Mamba runs
DM_HLN.minimum_detectable_effect_note_per_C-07: Pre-committed interpretation rule: a non-significant DM result (HLN p > 0.05) is reported as 'inconclusive' unless the bootstrap 95% CI on ΔIC has |upper_bound| < 0.005 (practically negligible threshold). Otherwise paper §Discussion frames as 'unable to detect difference at our sample size' NOT 'no difference exists'.

transaction_cost_ladder.cost_semantics_LOCKED_per_Codex_C-05: ONE-WAY per dollar traded (industry standard); round-trip cost is 2× listed value

position_construction_LOCKED_per_C-05.weights: EQUAL-WEIGHT (not score-weighted): position_i = +1/K if rank in top K, -1/K if bottom K, 0 otherwise
position_construction_LOCKED_per_C-05.overlap: non-overlapping (each fold yields 3-4 rebalance periods per horizon_ablation_results.csv n_periods column)

turnover_definition_LOCKED_per_D-05.primary: L1-norm: turnover_L1(t) = sum_i(|position_i(t) - position_i(t-1)|); counts BOTH entries and exits; at full rotation = 4 (long side 2 + short side 2)
turnover_definition_LOCKED_per_D-05.historical_alternative_in_existing_code: archived/scripts/run_horizon_ablation.py uses one-side definition: turnover_oneside(t) = (1 - |top_idx ∩ prev_long|/K) + (1 - |bot_idx ∩ prev_short|/K); counts ENTRIES only; at full rotation = 2; empirical mean from horizon_ablation_results.csv (horizon=21) is 1.77 → 88% of names rotate per period
turnover_definition_LOCKED_per_D-05.cost_formula_LOCKED: cost_per_period(t, c_bps) = turnover_L1(t) × c_bps × 1e-4 (no extra ×2 since L1 already counts both legs)
turnover_definition_LOCKED_per_D-05.implementation_warning: If reusing existing horizon_ablation Sharpe_net, either (a) recompute with turnover_L1 = 2 × existing_turnover, or (b) keep existing turnover_oneside but use cost = turnover_oneside × (2×c_bps) × 1e-4 to match the locked spec arithmetically. Prior v3 draft said 'turnover ≈ 2 at full rotation' which understated cost by ½; corrected per Codex Round D D-05.

transaction_cost_ladder.Net_Sharpe_formula: mean(net_period_return) / std(net_period_return) × sqrt(252/21)  -- annualization for 21d non-overlapping periods, NOT sqrt(252)

effective_sample_size_LOCKED_per_D-05.derivation: Sum of per-fold n_periods at horizon=21 from experiments/horizon_ablation_results.csv: fold 0 = 3, fold 1 = 4, fold 2 = 4, fold 3 = 3, fold 4 = 3 → total 17 periods per (model, universe, cost) configuration
effective_sample_size_LOCKED_per_D-05.prior_draft_error: v3 pre-D-05 said '5 folds × ~3 periods = 15'; underestimated; corrected per Codex Round D D-05 with empirical verification against CSV

transaction_cost_ladder.uncertainty_reporting: Report Net Sharpe as MEAN ± block-bootstrap CI (block_size=1 since 21d periods are non-overlapping, n_boot=5000); mark as SECONDARY metric due to small N=17; primary ranking comparison remains pooled per-day IC (T=313)
transaction_cost_ladder.extension_of: existing Sharpe_net column in archived/scripts/run_horizon_ablation.py — VERIFY equal-weight + L1-norm-turnover OR matched-arithmetic alternative before reuse (v3 spec is equal-weight + L1-norm; v2 plan formula was score-weighted, mismatch corrected per C-05; existing code uses one-side turnover, conversion documented per D-05)

dropped_post_processing_from_v2: DSR (Deflated Sharpe Ratio): dropped per H博士; industry-not-academic; Hansen SPA is academic-standard equivalent
PBO (Probability of Backtest Overfitting): dropped per H博士; v2 design split seeds not time (Codex A-03)
Romano-Wolf StepM: dropped per H博士; overkill given SPA + BH-FDR coverage
ONC clustering of N_eff: dropped (was input to DSR)
PIMP B=100+: dropped (excessive compute, qualitative result unchanged)

outputs.hp_grid_json: experiments/storya_e1_anchor/hp_grid.json (must mirror model_hparams in this prereg)

checkpoint_resume.rationale: Colab SSH unstable per H博士; disconnects every ~2h; manifest-based atomic resume saves ≤1 cell of work on disconnect
checkpoint_resume.implementation: Atomic append after each cell completion; --resume flag default ON; SIGTERM handler graceful flush

what_is_FORBIDDEN_under_this_v3_prereg: Any adaptive / observation-based seed extension (v1 forbidden; v3 reaffirms)
Changing n_seeds from 10 after pilot results seen
Adding LSTM back to E1 model list without filing v4 prereg + H博士 approval + Codex Round D review
Cherry-picking seeds by test IC values (data snooping)
Reporting top-K-by-test-IC as headline (cherry-pick red line per plan Decision Log)
Switching to different fold split or different feature universes after runs begin
Hyperparameter search beyond the locked grid in hp_grid.json
Adding extra models mid-run (e.g. inserting Transformer between models)
Running E1 cells without explicit purge/embargo per §1.8 v3 contract
Computing post-hoc statistics outside the locked SPA + DM/HLN + block bootstrap + cost ladder framework (e.g. resurrecting DSR or PBO)
allowed_under_this_v3_prereg: Reporting full distribution mean ± std + 95% block-bootstrap CI as headline (plan §1.1 reporting commitments)
Reporting top-10 by VAL IC ensemble test predictions as Supplementary 'Operational Deployment' table (defensible per ML standard, NOT cherry-pick)
Excluding seeds that failed to converge (best val IC < -0.05) as 'failed cells'; count and report all exclusions per-model per-universe
Hansen SPA + DM/HLN + block bootstrap + transaction cost ladder analysis post-processing per plan §1.4
E3 news-edge runs (plan §1.2) using same canonical 10 seeds + same hparams + same folds; α3 of E4-α references E3 outputs
E4-α clean edge ablation (plan §1.3) using same canonical 10 seeds + same hparams + same folds; α1/α3 reuse from E1-B and E3 respectively
Optional E5 Mamba ablation (plan §1.5) GO/SKIP gated by remaining budget after E1+E3+E4-α (decision based on time, NOT performance)

failure_exclusion_rule.criterion: best validation IC < -0.05 over patience window
failure_exclusion_rule.rationale: Same threshold as Plan AAA cell_id=28 handling; excludes obvious training failures, not low-IC results
failure_exclusion_rule.must_be_reported: Per-model per-universe count of excluded cells disclosed in paper Table 1; if exclusion rate > 20% for any (model, universe) cell, paper §Discussion must address

amendment_rule: Any change to this v3 prereg requires (a) explicit H博士 approval BEFORE the change, (b) prereg_v4.json filed with explicit diff from v3, (c) Codex Round D review on the amended plan, (d) the change reason logged in progress.md naming the prereg version that produced any subsequent results.

design_changes_from_v2.models: 5 (including LSTM, Mamba-SAGE as core) → 4 (drop LSTM; Mamba-SAGE moved to OPTIONAL E5 separate prereg)
design_changes_from_v2.universes: Universe B only → Universe B + C paired anchor
design_changes_from_v2.post_processing_primary: DSR + PBO + BH-FDR → Hansen SPA + DM/HLN + BH-FDR

what_v3_preserves_from_v2: Pre-committed fixed sample sizes (no observation-based extension)
Cherry-pick red line: no test-IC cherry-pick anywhere; full distribution reporting
Block bootstrap CI as supporting evidence
BH-FDR multiple-testing correction over pairwise family
Codex Round A finding dispositions (5 FIXED + 4 STILL-OPEN + 2 ACCEPTED-AS-CONCERN)
Per-day IC array saving for downstream block bootstrap + DM/HLN
v3_simplification_summary.why_v3_simpler_is_better: v2 was complex due to Codex Round A pressure (adaptive design + DSR + PBO + multi-testing ledger). H博士 evening review noted: (a) cherry-pick risk already eliminated by reporting full distribution + SPA, no need for DSR (industry-only) or PBO (wrong axis per A-03); (b) tiered 100/100/30/30/30 was overkill (10 seeds gives SE ≈ 0.0033 which is sufficient for headline IC reporting); (c) LSTM cannot align cleanly with rolling-stats Universe B/C; (d) compute reduction (1450 → 400 cells) frees budget for E3 news-edge + E4-α clean ablation + E6 SPA/DM/cost post-processing. v3 is methodologically more defensible AND faster AND simpler to implement.
